description: Canonical runtime DB data-plane contract. Table families, not individual rows, define ownership, data class, retention, deletion, and trust boundaries.

table_families.primary_access_path: vault setup, unlock-status, wrapper selection, and account deletion services
table_families.retention_policy: Retain while account exists; delete on account deletion or explicit vault reset flow.
table_families.deletion_behavior: Hard-delete with account deletion after dependent personal data is purged.
table_families.trust_boundary: Vault key material and wrappers remain encrypted or hashed; raw vault keys never persist server-side.

table_families.primary_access_path: account, persona, notification, verified email alias, and identity-cache services
table_families.retention_policy: Retain while account exists; refresh from authoritative identity sources where possible.
table_families.deletion_behavior: Delete or invalidate on account deletion; unregister push tokens on sign-out/delete.
table_families.trust_boundary: Metadata only; no vault keys, passphrases, tokens, or decrypted PKM.

table_families.primary_access_path: consent issuance, revocation, notification, and internal access review
table_families.retention_policy: Long-retention metadata audit; redact payload detail rather than deleting audit authority rows.
table_families.deletion_behavior: Account export may include safe audit metadata; deletion redacts user linkage only if legally required.
table_families.trust_boundary: Audit metadata only; signed-in is not consent and audit rows do not grant authority by themselves.

table_families.primary_access_path: scoped export generation, connector key wrapping, and refresh jobs
table_families.retention_policy: Short-lived by export expiry; stale refresh jobs compacted after terminal status.
table_families.deletion_behavior: Delete on account deletion and expire/revoke when the consent grant expires.
table_families.trust_boundary: Encrypted export payloads and wrapped key metadata only; no raw export keys or decrypted PKM.

table_families.primary_access_path: PKM store/read/delete domain routes and client-side vault unlock flows
table_families.retention_policy: Retain until account deletion or explicit domain deletion.
table_families.deletion_behavior: Delete by user and domain for domain deletion; delete all user rows on account deletion.
table_families.trust_boundary: Ciphertext segments only; backend does not inspect plaintext PKM payloads.

table_families.primary_access_path: PKM metadata, scope discovery, manifest repair, and replay/audit services
table_families.retention_policy: Retain while account exists; compact high-volume event metadata after replay window if safe.
table_families.deletion_behavior: Delete all user rows on account deletion; domain delete removes domain-scoped rows.
table_families.trust_boundary: Queryable metadata and scope handles only; no raw decrypted PKM.

table_families.primary_access_path: client-side PKM cutover and generic PKM upgrade orchestration
table_families.retention_policy: Retain active and recent terminal runs; compact terminal rows after 30 days unless needed for incident review.
table_families.deletion_behavior: Delete all user rows on account deletion.
table_families.trust_boundary: Run/checkpoint metadata only; decrypt/transform/re-encrypt remains client-side after vault unlock.

table_families.primary_access_path: bounded legacy-to-PKM compatibility and account deletion cleanup only
table_families.retention_policy: No new canonical writes; delete after PKM cutover evidence proves no active dependency.
table_families.deletion_behavior: Account deletion may remove legacy rows; new product features must not target these tables.
table_families.trust_boundary: Legacy ciphertext/metadata only; migration adapter remains time-boxed.

table_families.primary_access_path: legacy/domain reference compatibility during PKM scope migration
table_families.retention_policy: Retain until PKM manifests and scope registry fully replace runtime domain classification.
table_families.deletion_behavior: Reference cleanup only after migration plan; not user-delete scoped.
table_families.trust_boundary: Reference metadata only; not source of private PKM truth.

table_families.primary_access_path: RIA profile, marketplace, client relationship, invite, and share-grant services
table_families.retention_policy: Retain while relationship/profile exists; expire or disconnect relationship-scoped grants.
table_families.deletion_behavior: Delete or disconnect user-scoped relationships on account/persona deletion.
table_families.trust_boundary: Professional and relationship metadata only; no decrypted investor PKM without consent export.

table_families.primary_access_path: Plaid link, refresh, portfolio source preference, and portfolio-read services
table_families.retention_policy: Short-lived provider cache; refresh or purge stale derived state and keep encrypted provider tokens only as required.
table_families.deletion_behavior: Delete on disconnect/account deletion; revoke provider access where supported.
table_families.trust_boundary: Encrypted provider tokens and bounded derived state; not durable PKM unless consented and re-written into PKM.

table_families.primary_access_path: user-consented Gmail receipt sync and receipt-memory preview services
table_families.retention_policy: Short-lived sync and preview cache; durable memory requires explicit PKM write through consent/vault path.
table_families.deletion_behavior: Delete on Gmail disconnect/account deletion; compact sync runs and preview artifacts.
table_families.trust_boundary: Encrypted OAuth tokens, receipt summaries, hashes, and preview artifacts; no raw mailbox memory.

table_families.primary_access_path: market/reference lookup, cache refresh, and Kai market insight services
table_families.retention_policy: Refreshable shared reference/cache data; expire market cache by freshness windows.
table_families.deletion_behavior: Not user-delete scoped; rebuild from providers or seed/reference sources.
table_families.trust_boundary: Shared market/reference data only; no user-private PKM.

table_families.primary_access_path: Alpaca/Plaid funding, ACH, transfer, webhook, reconciliation, and trade-intent services
table_families.retention_policy: Long-retention operational/audit metadata for money movement and trade execution.
table_families.deletion_behavior: Do not hard-delete regulated operational evidence without explicit legal/compliance process.
table_families.trust_boundary: Execution metadata and encrypted provider/account references; no plaintext secrets or raw vault data.

table_families.primary_access_path: One mailbox intake, KYC workflow status, per-user public client connector metadata, consent continuation, transient approved Gmail send, and encrypted PKM writeback receipt
table_families.retention_policy: Active workflow metadata retained while open; legacy dev/UAT draft_body rows are redacted by migration and terminal metadata is compacted by retention jobs.
table_families.deletion_behavior: Delete user-linked workflows on account deletion; keep mailbox watch state without user data.
table_families.trust_boundary: Metadata only plus public connector metadata and send/writeback hashes; no raw full email threads, connector private keys, consent export plaintext, vault contents, broad decrypted PKM, or review draft plaintext. Approved outgoing email body may be handled transiently only for Gmail send and must not be logged or persisted.

table_families.primary_access_path: developer app registration, token issuance, and MCP/developer API access
table_families.retention_policy: Retain active apps/tokens and audit metadata; revoke or expire inactive tokens.
table_families.deletion_behavior: Delete/revoke by developer app lifecycle; preserve audit metadata where needed.
table_families.trust_boundary: Developer metadata and token hashes/wrappers only; no user PKM payloads.
